title: Anneslia mollis
intro: Anneslia mollis, commonly known as softstem Anneslia or softstem Anneslia mollis, is a plant species in the family Malpighiaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine, offering various health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiparasitic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Anneslia mollis contains bioactive constituents including flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Anneslia mollis is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to treat respiratory infections, fever, and other ailments.
taxonomy: Anneslia mollis is classified under the family Annonaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the custard apple family. This classification places Anneslia mollis within the order Magnoliales, a group of flowering plants that are known for their distinctive flowers and fruits. The family Annonaceae is part of the larger clade Magnoliidae, which includes a diverse range of flowering plant families. Anneslia mollis is a species of flowering plant that is native to tropical regions of Central and South America. Further classification of Anneslia mollis may also include its placement within the genus Anneslia, which is a small group of flowering plants.

Anneslia mollis has been traditionally used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions.
